mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarter-over-quarter increase in real GDP growth expands corporate earnings expectations, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in positive GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below the 7.0% threshold signals labor market stabilization, reducing recession risk premiums and supporting equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in >60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 7.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and retail sales revenue indicate broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive equity returns over a six-week horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals produce negative SPX 60d returns in more than half of observed episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound environment, further reductions in the federal funds rate signal aggressive policy accommodation that compresses discount rates and boosts equity valuations within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when FEDFUNDS declines below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production increase exceeding 1.5% confirms manufacturing sector resilience, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as supply constraints ease.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth above 1.5% correlates with negative SPX 20d returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reinforces long-term economic expansion narratives, supporting equity valuations over a three-month forward window.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth months consistently yield negative SPX 60d returns across multiple decades.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a rising federal funds rate indicate tightening monetary policy that outpaces inflation, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Simultaneous CPI and FEDFUNDS increases fail to produce negative SPX 20d returns in >60% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in real GDP coinciding with a rising unemployment rate confirms a deepening recession, triggering severe equity drawdowns over the subsequent month as earnings forecasts collapse.
failure_condition: GDP contractions above 2.0% with rising unemployment do not produce negative SPX 20d returns in >70% of observed episodes.
